12. In “Pritam Pal vs. High Court of Madhya Pradesh, Jabalpur, 
through Registrar”14 the following is held: 
“15. Prior to the Contempt of Courts Act, 1971, it was 
held that the High Court has inherent power to deal 
with a contempt of itself summarily and to adopt its own 
procedure, provided that it gives a fair and reasonable 
opportunity to the contemnor to defend himself. But the 
procedure has now been prescribed by Section 15 of the 
Act in exercise of the powers conferred by Entry 14, List 
III of the Seventh Schedule of the Constitution. Though
